Feature:AMO001 Get Member Wallet Balance 103ms 5

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant wallet balance API
So that I can retrieve the member wallet balance for one or more currencies

Scenario:
Get balances for requested currencies
46ms 4

Given a merchant member exists 1ms Show Info + 
Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Info 

Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: 44ms
field value
platform_username <platform_username>
currencies ["CNY","THB"]
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY",
            "THB"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": -4998.396544,
                "THB": 0
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Info 

Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY",
            "THB"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": -4998.396544,
                "THB": 0
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Then the AMO001 response should be successful < 1ms

And the response should contain balances for: < 1ms
field value
platform_username <platform_username>
currencies ["CNY","THB"]

Setup:

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: 11ms
field value
platform_username
currencies ["CNY","THB"]
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY",
            "THB"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "platform_username",
                "message": "\"platform_username\" is not allowed to be empty"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

Info 

Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance

Request:

    Payload:

          "currencies": [
            "CNY",

          "status": 404,

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: 13ms
field value
platform_username <platform_username>
currencies ["USD","MYR","INVALID"]
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "USD",
            "MYR",
            "INVALID"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "currencies.1",
                "message": "Each currency must be one of: CNY,VND,USDT,THB,TRY,INR,AUD,PHP,BRL,MXN,NPR,BDT,IDR,KRW,JPY,HKD,PKR,RON,DKK,NOK,MMK,TWD,CUP,USD,EUR,GBP,CHF,CAD,RUB,KES,KKC,LUCK,MYX"
              },
              {
                "key": "currencies.2",
                "message": "Each currency must be one of: CNY,VND,USDT,THB,TRY,INR,AUD,PHP,BRL,MXN,NPR,BDT,IDR,KRW,JPY,HKD,PKR,RON,DKK,NOK,MMK,TWD,CUP,USD,EUR,GBP,CHF,CAD,RUB,KES,KKC,LUCK,MYX"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

Info

          ]
        }

Feature:AMO013 Notify Wager Update < 1ms 3

As APISYS
I want to notify the merchant of wager updates

Scenario:
Successful wager notification
< 1ms 2

When APISYS notifies wager update with: < 1ms 

{
  "notification_type": "typeA",
  "notifications": [
    {
      "game_type": "IMSPORT",
      "game_key": "IMSPORT",
      "wager_no": "0001-20250117140015-188011927396-3",
      "origin_wager_no": "0001-20250117140015-188011927396-1",
      "ticket_no": "ticketno_123",
      "platform_username": "member01",
      "type": 3,
      "status": 2,
      "currency": "CNY",
      "amount": 10.1,
      "payment_amount": 10.1,
      "effective_amount": 10,
      "profit_and_loss": 5.1,
      "wager_time": 1720694402,
      "settlement_time": 1720698600,
      "is_system_reward": true
    },
    {
      "game_type": "CRASH",
      "game_key": "CRASH_V2",
      "wager_no": "0002-20250117140015-188011927398-1",
      "origin_wager_no": null,
      "ticket_no": null,
      "platform_username": "member02",
      "type": 1,
      "status": 2,
      "currency": "CNY",
      "amount": 5.1,
      "payment_amount": 5.1,
      "effective_amount": 5,
      "profit_and_loss": 0,
      "wager_time": 1720694403,
      "settlement_time": null,
      "is_system_reward": false
    }
  ]
}
 

Then(/^APISYS notifies wager update with:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Then the AMO013 response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the AMO013 response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": -4998.396544
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Feature:AMO009 Seamless Resettle Wager 35ms 2

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant resettle wager API
So that I can apply corrected settlement amounts

Scenario:
Resettlement increases the wallet balance
16ms 2 2 2

Given a merchant member exists < 1ms Show Info + 
Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Info 

Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Given I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>" 15ms Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": -4998.396544
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }
Balance:
    CNY:
        -4998.396544

Info

    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance

When APISYS resettles a wager with: < 1ms
field value
transaction_no <transaction_no>
currency <currency>
amount 12.5 

Then(/^APISYS resettles a wager with:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Then the AMO009 response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the AMO009 response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the response should contain: < 1ms
field value
reference_id any non-empty value

And the wallet balance in "<currency>" should increase by 12.5 < 1ms

Feature:Integration Flow - Request payment then cancel wager < 1ms 1

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant wallet APIs in sequence
So that I can verify paid wager cancellation refund flow end to end

Scenario:
Request payment 30 then cancel wager refunds the same 30
< 1ms 10 1

Given a merchant member exists with platform username "<platform_username>" < 1ms 

Then(/^a merchant member exists with platform username "([^"]*)"$/, function(arg1, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions

});

currency balance
<currency> 100 

Then(/^the member wallet has balances:$/, function(callback) {

  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Given a bet exists with: < 1ms
wager_no
parent_wager_no <parent_wager_no> 

Then(/^a bet exists with:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests payment with: < 1ms
transaction_no
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount -30

Then the response should be successful < 1ms

  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And a request payment transaction exists with: < 1ms
wager_no
order_no <transaction_uuid_1>
currency <currency>
amount -30

  callback(null, 'pending');
});

wager_no
payment_amount 30

  callback(null, 'pending');
});

amount 75.125
session_id <session_id>

    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "e95e303c-20c7-4666-bcbf-a40cdcbbdeee",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "75.125",
          "session_id": "10a56450-7b06-4b06-94cf-8d889b55e01f"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "reference_id": "e95d8be9-b700-4de2-935d-ea01603e3c26",
              "status": 1
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": -4873.271544
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-in
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "20a5427e-5cf7-42fd-8929-b9fee1831c88",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "1.1234567",
          "session_id": "ee8f7601-9f52-4626-8d0f-3cc23995e439"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "amount",
                "message": "Amount must be a number with maximum 6 decimal places"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

session_id <session_id>

field value
reference_id any non-empty value
amount -15
status 1

field value

game_type <game_type>

Then the AMO011 response should be successful < 1ms

field value
reference_id <amo011_reference_id>
amount -15
status 1

Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "3df59297-05f3-4b28-9f98-1904a91346f8",

Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": -4853.271544
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

And I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>" < 1ms

When APISYS requests transfer out with: < 1ms
field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>
game_type <game_type>
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
session_id <session_id>

Then the AMO011 response should be successful < 1ms

And the response should contain: < 1ms
field value
reference_id any non-empty value
status 1

And the response amount should equal the integer part of the recorded wallet balance in "<currency>" < 1ms

And the wallet balance in "<currency>" should equal the remaining decimal balance < 1ms

When APISYS requests transfer out with: 12ms
field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>
game_type <game_type>
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount -1.1234567
session_id <session_id>
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "d2e931ba-5114-459b-8e13-ddeb56386f01",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "-1.1234567",
          "session_id": "4f00e9db-0b2d-4d8e-a2f9-46d0851636d5"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "amount",
                "message": "Amount must be a number with maximum 6 decimal places"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

          "transfer_no": "c3ff0cc5-eca6-4e83-8da6-8691dd12c83f",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "amount": "-10",
          "session_id": "4d3fb87f-ba2b-4eae-bd9f-6f6cfa9bf945"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "currency",
                "message": "Currency is required"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

Feature:Integration Flow - Transfer out all integer balance < 1ms 1

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant transfer APIs in sequence
So that I can verify full integer-balance withdrawal behavior end to end

Scenario:
Transfer in 120.5 then transfer out without amount deducts 120 and leaves 0.5
< 1ms 7 4

Given a merchant member exists with platform username "<platform_username>" < 1ms 

Then(/^a merchant member exists with platform username "([^"]*)"$/, function(arg1, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests transfer in with: < 1ms
transfer_no
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount 120.5

Then the response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 120.5 < 1ms 

Then(/^the wallet balance for "([^"]*)" in "([^"]*)" should become 120.5$/, function(arg1, arg2, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests transfer out with: < 1ms
transfer_no
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>

Then the response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the response should contain: < 1ms
status success
amount 120

And the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 0.5 < 1ms 

Then(/^the wallet balance for "([^"]*)" in "([^"]*)" should become 0.5$/, function(arg1, arg2, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: < 1ms
platform_username
currencies <currency>

Then the response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the response should contain balances: < 1ms
0.5 

Then(/^the response should contain balances:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Feature:Integration Flow - Transfer lifecycle through merchant wallet APIs < 1ms 1

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant wallet APIs in sequence
So that I can verify transfer credit, debit, reversal, and balance retrieval end to end

Scenario:
Transfer in 100, get balance, transfer out 35, get balance, cancel transfer, get balance
< 1ms 14 8

Given a merchant member exists with platform username "<platform_username>" < 1ms 

Then(/^a merchant member exists with platform username "([^"]*)"$/, function(arg1, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests transfer in with: < 1ms
transfer_no
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount 100

Then the response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the response should contain: < 1ms
status success

And the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 100 < 1ms 

Then(/^the wallet balance for "([^"]*)" in "([^"]*)" should become 100$/, function(arg1, arg2, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: < 1ms
platform_username
currencies <currency>

Then the response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the response should contain balances: < 1ms
100 

Then(/^the response should contain balances:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

transfer_no
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount -35

  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');

status success
amount -35
